source.title: Accelerated directed evolution of dye-decolorizing peroxidase using a bacterial extracellular protein secretion system (BENNY)

initial_request.research_problem: Enhance H2O2 tolerance of dye-decolorizing peroxidase 4 (DyP4) from Pleurotus ostreatus using directed evolution, accelerated by a bacterial extracellular protein secretion system (BENNY).
initial_request.known_context: DyP4 is a haem peroxidase with potential for lignin valorization but is sensitive to H2O2 inactivation. Methionine oxidation was hypothesized as a cause.
constraints: Must maintain proper folding and activity
input_data: Crystal structure of DyP4 F194Y variant (PDB 6FSK)

evolution_trajectory.state_before: DyP4 is sensitive to H2O2, limiting its application.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Methionine oxidation may cause H2O2-mediated inactivation.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Substituting methionine residues (M43, M77, M253) with leucine or phenylalanine will improve H2O2 tolerance.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Create methionine-substituted variants (M43L, M77L, M253L, M253F) to test the hypothesis.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Variants need to be tested for H2O2 tolerance.

evidence.quote_or_span: In an attempt to enhance the $H_{2}O_{2}$ tolerance of DyP4, we created 4 methionine-substituted variants (M43L M77L M253L and M253F).

To construct M43L (primers M43L-F and M43L-R), M253L (primers M253L-F and M253L-R) and M253F (primers M253F-F and M253F-R) variants, partially overlapping primers and Q5 high-fidelity DNA polymerase (New England Biolabs) were used in a 2-stage PCR.
To construct M77L variant (primers M77L-F and M77L-R), DNA polymerase used was substituted with PfuUltra high-fidelity DNA polymerase AD (Agilent Technologies).
All methionine-substituted variants were created using pET-24a(+)-DyP4 as template.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Methionine-substituted variants created but not yet characterized for H2O2 tolerance.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether methionine substitution improves H2O2 tolerance is unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Methionine substitution will increase H2O2 tolerance.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test H2O2 tolerance of M43L, M77L, M253L, M253F variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: All variants were properly folded and active, but none showed improved H2O2 tolerance.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Rational design failed; switch to directed evolution approach.

evidence.quote_or_span: In an attempt to enhance the $H_{2}O_{2}$ tolerance of DyP4, we created 4 methionine-substituted variants (M43L M77L M253L and M253F).

To construct M43L (primers M43L-F and M43L-R), M253L (primers M253L-F and M253L-R) and M253F (primers M253F-F and M253F-R) variants, partially overlapping primers and Q5 high-fidelity DNA polymerase (New England Biolabs) were used in a 2-stage PCR.
To construct M77L variant (primers M77L-F and M77L-R), DNA polymerase used was substituted with PfuUltra high-fidelity DNA polymerase AD (Agilent Technologies).
All methionine-substituted variants were created using pET-24a(+)-DyP4 as template.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Rational methionine substitution failed to improve H2O2 tolerance.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a high-throughput method to screen for improved variants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: An OsmY-based bacterial extracellular protein secretion system (BENNY) can streamline directed evolution by enabling extracellular secretion and bypassing cell lysis.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design and construct OsmY-DyP4 fusion for secretion in E. coli BL21(DE3).
evolution_trajectory.observation: OsmY-DyP4 fusion successfully secreted into culture medium, bypassing induction and cell disruption.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate the BENNY-assisted assay for high-throughput screening.

evidence.quote_or_span: To accelerate DyP4 evolution, we used a bacterial extracellular protein secretion system (BENNY) based on the Escherichia coli osmotically-inducible protein Y (OsmY).
this is the first report of applying OsmY to streamline directed evolution workflow and to direct extracellular secretion of a haem protein such as DyP4.
To streamline the DyP4 directed evolution workflow, we fused E. coli OsmY to the N-terminus of DyP4 and investigated protein secretion
By using OsmY as secretory carrier and fusion protein expression in AIM, we bypassed both (i) protein induction (i.e. addition of IPTG inducer) and (ii) cell disruption

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BENNY system designed but not validated for high-throughput screening.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to assess assay variability and edge effects in 96-well format.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: BENNY-assisted ABTS assay in 96-well plates will have acceptable variability for screening.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Validate assay by measuring coefficient of variance (CV) and edge effects.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Apparent CV 8%, true CV 22% (11% excluding border wells); edge effects identified.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Assay is suitable for screening with precautions for edge effects; proceed to library design.

evidence.quote_or_span: Based on ABTS assay, we obtained an apparent coefficient of variance (CV) of 8% for OsmY-DyP4
If all bordering wells were excluded, the apparent and true CVs were reduced to 4% and 11%, respectively.
In the 1st and 2nd rounds, we screened 558 clones [2 96-well plates from each epPCR condition (H, M, L)].
coli as protein expression host, protein variants are often expressed in 96-well format.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Validated BENNY-assisted assay ready for screening.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to generate diverse mutant libraries for directed evolution.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Three epPCR conditions (H, M, L) with varying error rates will produce libraries with a range of mutation frequencies suitable for directed evolution.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design epPCR conditions targeting only DyP4 sequence.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Three epPCR conditions designed to generate libraries with high, medium, and low mutation rates.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Use these conditions to generate libraries for screening.

evidence.quote_or_span: Three error-prone polymerase chain reaction (epPCR) conditions were used in this study: high (H), medium (M) and low (L) error rates.
we created random mutagenesis libraries of OsmY-DyP4 using 3 epPCR conditions (H, M and L).
mutations were only targeted to the sequence encoding for DyP4, leaving the OsmY sequence unaltered.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to screen for variants with improved H2O2 tolerance.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Screening at 4 mM H2O2 (16× optimal) will select for variants with enhanced tolerance.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform 3 rounds of epPCR screening with increasing H2O2 concentration (4 mM) and use best variants as parents for next round.

tool_or_method.name: Directed evolution by epPCR and screening

evolution_trajectory.observation: Screening yielded progressively improved variants; best variants OsmY-1D2 (round 1), OsmY-2A5 (round 2), and OsmY-3F6 (round 3) were identified.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Use best epPCR variant (3F6) as template for saturation mutagenesis.

evidence.quote_or_span: In the 1st and 2nd rounds, we screened 558 clones [2 96-well plates from each epPCR condition (H, M, L)].

Worthy of note, in order to isolate OsmY-DyP4 variants of enhanced $H_{2}O_{2}$ tolerance, we intentionally increased the $H_{2}O_{2}$ concentration in our assay from 0.25 to 4 mM, representing a 16× increase.
In the 3rd round, 1116 clones [4 96-well plates from each epPCR condition (H, M, L)] were screened.
The best variant from each epPCR round (OsmY-1D2 from 1st round and OsmY-2A5 from 2nd round) was used as parental template for the subsequent epPCR round.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify mutations in 4D4 and assess their structural context.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: 4D4 carries beneficial mutations that improve H2O2 tolerance and activity.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 4D4 has 4 missense mutations (I56V, K109R, N227S, N312S) and 4 silent mutations; average 1.82 nucleotide substitutions per gene, 63.6% AT→GC transitions; mutations not near haem; K109R and N312S increase H-bonds.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Remove OsmY to characterize mutations independently.

evidence.quote_or_span: On average, there were 1.82 nucleotide substitutions per DyP4 gene
None of these residues is in the vicinity of haem.
None of these residues is in the vicinity of haem.
K109R and N312S substitutions resulted in higher number of H-bonds formed (3 H-bonds in R109 vs. 1 H-bond in K109 and 4 H-bonds in S312 vs. 3 H-bonds in N312).

Result: After 3 rounds of random mutagenesis with error-prone polymerase chain reaction (epPCR) and 1 round of saturation mutagenesis, we obtained 4D4 variant (I56V, K109R, N227S and N312S) that displays multiple desirable phenotypes, including higher protein yield and secretion, higher specific activity (2.7-fold improvement in $k_{cat}/K_{m}$ ) and higher $H_{2}O_{2}$ tolerance (sevenfold improvement based on $IC_{50}$ ).

Result: After 3 rounds of random mutagenesis with error-prone polymerase chain reaction (epPCR) and 1 round of saturation mutagenesis, we obtained 4D4 variant (I56V, K109R, N227S and N312S) that displays multiple desirable phenotypes, including higher protein yield and secretion, higher specific activity (2.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: 4D4 mutations identified but fused to OsmY.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to determine if mutations alone improve protein yield and activity.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Removing OsmY will allow characterization of the mutations' effects on yield and kinetics.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Remove N-terminal OsmY from WT, 3F6, and 4D4, express and purify proteins.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Protein yields: WT 1.16 mg, 3F6 1.20 mg, 4D4 2.70 mg from 200 mL culture.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Perform kinetic analysis on purified proteins.

evidence.quote_or_span: To determine the effects of mutations found, we removed the N-terminal OsmY of WT, 3F6 and 4D4, expressed and purified these 3 proteins, and performed further characterizations.
From 200-mL shake flask cultures, the estimated amounts of purified protein obtained were 1.16 mg for WT, 1.20 mg for 3F6 and 2.70 mg for 4D4.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to quantify catalytic efficiency improvements.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: 3F6 and 4D4 have improved kcat/Km compared to WT.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure kinetic constants for ABTS oxidation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: kcat/Km: WT 4.31, 3F6 11.78, 4D4 11.60 mM-1 s-1; 2.7-fold improvement for both variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: The combination of higher $k_{cat}$ and lower $K_{m}$ resulted in a 2.7-fold improvement in specific activity for both 3F6 and 4D4.
Variants 3F6 and 4D4 showed lower $K_{m}$ values of 0.427 mM and 0.
The best variant from each epPCR round (OsmY-1D2 from 1st round and OsmY-2A5 from 2nd round) was used as parental template for the subsequent epPCR round.
In the 1st and 2nd rounds, we screened 558 clones [2 96-well plates from each epPCR condition (H, M, L)].

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to quantify H2O2 tolerance improvement.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: 4D4 has significantly improved H2O2 tolerance compared to WT.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure IC50 for H2O2 using ABTS assay.
evolution_trajectory.observation: IC50: WT 0.97 mM, 3F6 4.67 mM, 4D4 7.03 mM; 7-fold improvement for 4D4 over WT.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Identify limitations of the BENNY system.

evidence.quote_or_span: the $IC_{50}$ value was increased from 0.97 mM (WT) to 4.67 mM (3F6) and 7.03 mM (4D4)
the $H_{2}O_{2}$ tolerance of 4D4 was improved 7×, comparing its $IC_{50}$ value to that of WT.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: 4D4 shows improved yield, kinetics, and H2O2 tolerance.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: BENNY system has a secretion bottleneck.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Protein production rate exceeds secretion rate, leading to intracellular accumulation and proteolytic cleavage of OsmY signal peptide.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze secretion efficiency and identify limitations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Production rate faster than secretion; signal peptide (positions 1–28) cleaved, preventing secretion of truncated protein.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Limitation motivates ongoing engineering of 4D4 and BENNY toolbox.

evidence.quote_or_span: protein production rate of OsmY-DyP4 is likely faster than its extracellular secretion rate
the signal peptide of OsmY (positions 1–28) was proteolytically cleaved, resulting in no secretion of this truncated protein pool.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Further engineering needed for lignin oxidation and broader BENNY applications.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: 4D4 can be further engineered for oxidation of S-type phenolic lignin units, and the BENNY toolbox can be expanded.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Initiate ongoing work to engineer 4D4 for S-type phenolic lignin units and expand BENNY toolbox.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Ongoing work to further engineer 4D4 for oxidation of S-type phenolic lignin units and expand BENNY toolbox.

evidence.quote_or_span: We are now further engineering 4D4 variant for the oxidation of S-type phenolic lignin units

success_verification.final_conclusion: Directed evolution using BENNY yielded variant 4D4 with 2.7-fold improved kcat/Km and 7-fold improved H2O2 tolerance (IC50 7.03 mM vs WT 0.97 mM), along with higher protein yield. BENNY system enabled high-throughput screening but has a secretion bottleneck.
limitations: Secretion bottleneck: production rate > secretion rate, signal peptide cleavage
Mutations not near haem; mechanism of tolerance improvement not fully elucidated
